title: 🤖 Copilot Adoption [Edu] [v2]
description: Tracks GitHub Copilot seat provisioning, activation rates, and daily active user trends across the organization.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'live' THEN '📡 Live Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    WHEN 'demo' THEN '🎮 Demo Environment — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '❓ Unknown Mode'
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 🤖 Copilot Adoption & Usage

**Supplementary Metrics — Copilot Seat Utilisation**

## What it Measures
This dashboard tracks how broadly and actively GitHub Copilot is being used across your organisation. It covers seat allocation, activation rates, and 28-day active usage — giving you a clear picture of Copilot adoption health.

## Why it Matters
Seat utilisation directly affects ROI. Inactive seats represent wasted spend and missed productivity opportunities. Understanding *who* is using Copilot and *how often* helps managers target enablement efforts and coaches identify developers who may need onboarding support.

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Seat Utilisation Rate** | ≥80% active in 28d | <50% — investigate blockers or low awareness |
| **Inactive Seats (>14d)** | Close to 0 | Rising count — reach out to inactive users |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces used | Single surface — expand use-case awareness |

panels.description: Copilot Adoption & Usage — seat utilisation, active users, and feature adoption.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Total count of all rows in `copilot_seats` at sync time. Point-in-time snapshot — no historical data. Multiple GitHub orgs require separate API calls. A 403 during sync → table empty with no error surfaced to dashboards.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Total Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_at` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Count of seats where `last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'`. Any Copilot interaction qualifies as 'active' — including rejected suggestions. The 28-day window is hardcoded (always relative to now), not aligned to the Grafana time picker.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats (28d)" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Ratio of active seats to total seats using `COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ...)` syntax. The numerator uses a rolling `NOW()-28d` window; the denominator uses the current total. Newly provisioned seats appear in the denominator immediately, before any activity.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days')::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1
) AS "Seat Utilization Rate (%)" FROM copilot_seats
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Seats where `last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days' OR last_activity_at IS NULL`. The 14-day threshold is arbitrary — developers on vacation are flagged as inactive. GitHub only allows seat reassignment at end of billing cycle.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Inactive Seats (>14d)"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days'
   OR last_activity_at IS NULL
```

panels.title: Daily Active Users

targets.rawSql:
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `daily_active_users` field | Tables: `copilot_org_metrics`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-metrics)

Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/organization-28-day/latest` (2026-03-10) → NDJSON → `daily_active_users` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_org_metrics`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Time series of `daily_active_users` from the org-level 28-day report. **Two-step fetch required:** (1) call the API endpoint to get `download_links`, (2) fetch the signed S3 URL WITHOUT auth headers, (3) parse each line as a separate JSON object (NDJSON). Only covers the last 28 days from today.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT day AS time, daily_active_users AS "Daily Active"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.title: Last Active Editor Distribution - Learning Guide

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats` → `last_activity_editor` field

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Bar chart of seat counts grouped by `last_activity_editor`. Records the **last** editor used, not the primary editor. Editor strings may include version numbers ('vscode/1.80.0'), which creates separate bars for the same editor.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT last_activity_editor AS "Editor", COUNT(*) AS "Seats"
FROM copilot_seats
WHERE last_activity_editor IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
```

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

All seat holders sorted by `last_activity_at` descending. Shows only the most-recent activity per seat — no activity history. `plan_type` column shows Individual / Business / Enterprise, which affects feature entitlements.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  assignee_login AS "Developer",
  last_activity_at AS "Last Active",
  last_activity_editor AS "Editor",
  plan_type AS "Plan"
FROM copilot_seats
ORDER BY last_activity_at DESC
```

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats

panels.title: Last Successful Sync

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'

panels.description: Source: Internal `sync_jobs` table (no GitHub API call) | Tables: `sync_jobs`

options.content:
### 📡 GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-user-management)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/seats`

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`copilot_seats`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

Row count of `copilot_seats` table — a data health indicator. If 0, the last sync did not load seat data (check server logs for a silent 403). The table is fully replaced on every sync (TRUNCATE + INSERT), unlike PRs and deployments (incremental).

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Copilot Seats" FROM copilot_seats
```

### 📡 GitHub API Source

Internal `sync_jobs` table (no GitHub API call)

### 🗄️ Database Table(s)

`sync_jobs`

### 📐 How It's Calculated

`MAX(finished_at)` from `sync_jobs` where `status='success'`. A 'success' status does NOT guarantee all resources were fetched — individual fetchers can fail silently (e.g., 403 on Copilot seats) while the overall job still completes with 'success'.

### 🔍 SQL Query

```sql
SELECT MAX(finished_at) AS "Last Successful Sync" FROM sync_jobs WHERE status = 'success'
```
